{{Platform |Name=WonderSwan |Guid=3045-65 |Image=https://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/scale_super/0/4647/2954594-wonderswan-black-left.jpg |Deck=The WonderSwan was a Japan-only handheld game system that had a fairly large library of games and many accessories. |ShortName=WSWN |ReleaseDate=1999-03-01 |ReleaseDateType=Month |InstallBase=1,550,000 |OnlineSupport=No |OriginalPrice=49.99 |Manufacturer=Companies/Bandai_Co_Ltd }} <div id='imageData' data-json='{"infobox":{"file":"2954594-wonderswan-black-left.jpg","path":"0\/4647\/","mime":"image\/jpeg","sizes":"square_small,square_medium,scale_super,scale_medium,square_tiny,square_mini,scale_small,scale_avatar,square_avatar,scale_large,screen_medium,screen_kubrick,scale_tiny","caption":null},"background":{"file":"176117-wonderswan.jpg","path":"0\/25\/","mime":"image\/jpeg","sizes":"scale_medium,scale_super,square_mini,square_medium,square_small,screen_medium,square_avatar,scale_small,scale_large,scale_avatar,square_tiny,screen_kubrick,scale_tiny,screen_kubrick_wide,ignore_jpg_scale_small","caption":"WonderSwan"}}' />
==Overview == http://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/original/0/9825/1040508-641px_wonderswan__article_image.jpg The WonderSwan is a handheld video game console that was released on March 4, 1999. It was only available in [[Locations/Japan|Japan]]. The console was released by [[Companies/Bandai_Co_Ltd|Bandai]], with one of the lead designers being [[People/Gunpei_Yokoi|Gunpei Yokoi]] (famed creator of the [[Platforms/Game_Boy|Game Boy]] and producer of the [[Franchises/Metroid|Metroid]] franchise who was killed before the release of the system). The WonderSwan was created to try and compete with other industry leaders; the[[Platforms/Neo_Geo_Pocket|Neo Geo Pocket]] and the Game Boy. The [[Platforms/WonderSwan_Color|WonderSwan Color]]was eventually released, but many WonderSwan Color games are also compatible with the original WonderSwan. The WonderSwan was originally available in ten different colors.
The WonderSwan was designed to be played either vertically or horizontally. Games varied on this aspect, with a wide variety of games. The games for the system were primarily Japanese, as the console was designed for this market, but some still feature English as a language. Many popular RPGs were ported to the system, such as [[Games/Final_Fantasy|Final Fantasy]], [[Games/Final_Fantasy_II|Final Fantasy II]], [[Games/Final_Fantasy_IV|Final Fantasy IV]], and [[Games/Front_Mission|Front Mission]]. In addition, the WonderSwan did have its share of original games.
==Accessories == http://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/original/0/7465/1207074-wonderswan_wonderwitch_4.jpg The WonderSwan had a variety of accessories as well. This included a headphone adapter (which allowed the console to have surround sound), a link cable (for multiplayer games), rechargeable batteries (which required a special recharger), WonderWave (a communication adapter that could be used to exchange data with some [[Platforms/PlayStation|PlayStation]] games), MobileWonderGate (a game cartridge that had a web browser and email), WonderBorg (a robot that could be controlled via a WonderSwan cartridge), WonderWitch (a game development kit that included a programmable WonderSwan cartridge and Microsoft Windows compatibility), WonderCoin (an accessory that made the D-pad on the WonderSwan feel more like a directional stick), screen protectors, a case, and a screen light.
==Technical Specification== * CPU: 16-bit NEC V30 MZ processor, 3.072 MHz * Screen: FSTN reflective LCD, 224 x 144 pixels, 2.49 in. * Display: 512 characters per layer, 128 sprites * Graphics: 8-shade monochrome * Audio: 4-channel stereo sound, mono speaker or headphones, three volume settings: loud, medium, mute * Size: 74.3 mm x 121 mm x 24.3 mm * Weight: 93 g or 110 g (depending on whether or not there are batteries in it) * Power: 1 AA battery rechargeable batteries * Ports: Link port, headphone jack, cartridge port * Cartridge capacity: ROM/RAM max - 128 Mbit
==Where to Find the Console and Games== Rising Stuff ([http://www.risingstuff.com/store www.risingstuff.com/store]) is about the world's largest English-speaking distributor of WonderSwan games. They also have a forum dedicated to WonderSwan games and information: ([http://www.risingstuff.com/forums www.risingstuff.com/forums]). Other than that [http:/www.ebay.com eBay] often has them.

{{Platform |Name=WonderSwan Color |Guid=3045-54 |Aliases=SwanCrystal,WSC |Image=https://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/scale_super/0/25/176139-wswpeablu.jpg |Deck=A Handheld gaming device from Bandai. The system was noteworthy for supporting play on the system vertically or horizontally, depending on the particular game. |ShortName=WSC |ReleaseDate=2000-12-09 |ReleaseDateType=Month |InstallBase=1,100,000 |OnlineSupport=No |OriginalPrice=59.99 |Manufacturer=Companies/Bandai_Co_Ltd }} <div id='imageData' data-json='{"infobox":{"file":"176139-wswpeablu.jpg","path":"0\/25\/","mime":"image\/jpeg","sizes":"scale_medium,scale_super,square_mini,square_medium,square_small,screen_medium,square_avatar,scale_small,scale_large,scale_avatar,square_tiny,screen_kubrick,scale_tiny,ignore_jpg_scale_small","caption":"WonderSwan Color Box"},"background":{"file":"2555404-bandai_wonderswan_color_logo_1.png","path":"0\/1992\/","mime":"image\/png","sizes":"square_small,square_tiny,square_medium,screen_kubrick_wide,scale_tiny,scale_super,scale_medium,scale_avatar,square_mini,scale_large,scale_small,screen_kubrick,square_avatar,screen_medium","caption":""}}' />
==Overview== http://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/original/0/7465/958519-final_fantasy_ii.png The WonderSwan Color was released on December 30, 2000 (only in [[Locations/Japan|Japan]]). It was created by [[Companies/Bandai_Co_Ltd|Bandai]], and the successor to the original [[Platforms/WonderSwan|WonderSwan]]. In Japan, it was met with modest success and sales (especially considering [[Companies/Nintendo|Nintendo]]'s near-monopoly of the handheld market). The market share of the WonderSwan Color was approximately 8%, according to Bandai. Much of this success is credited to the low price of the handheld; ¥6800, or $59. The WonderSwan Color also had great third party support, with companies such as [[Companies/Squaresoft|Squaresoft]] (who ported many of the original Final Fantasy games to the WonderSwan Color). When the WonderSwan Color's successor, the [[SwanCrystal|SwanCrystal]], was released, Nintendo had regained support of Squaresoft, however. One of the unique features of the WonderSwan (all versions) was the fact that it could be played either horizontally or vertically. http://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/original/0/7465/958521-wswanc.jpg The original WonderSwan featured only black and white. Naturally, the WonderSwan Color had a color LCD screen (as well as several more upgrades). The WonderSwan Color is slightly larger (7 mm larger) and heavier (2 g heavier), contained more RAM (64k of RAM). The WonderSwan Color is backwards compatible with many of the original WonderSwan's game (as well as the WonderSwan being compatible with many of the WonderSwan Color's games).
Similar to the [[Platforms/Nintendo_DS|Nintendo DS]], before the WonderSwan Color can be used, the player must enter data about oneself, including birth date (year, month, and day), name, sex, and blood type. The name appears under Bandai's logo whenever the handheld console is turned on. The WonderSwan Color launched in several colors (five, in fact): Pearl Blue, Crystal Pink, Crystal Black, and Crystal Orange. In addition, several limited editions of the console were released. Also, the console had a Final Fantasy bundle, packaged with either the original [[Games/Final_Fantasy|Final Fantasy]] or [[Games/Final_Fantasy_II|Final Fantasy II]], and a Final Fantasy skinned WonderSwan Color.
==Technical Specifications== * CPU: SPGY-1002, 3.072 MHz 16-bit NEC V30MZ duplicate * Memory: 64 kB shared VRAM/WRAM * Screen: FSTN reflective LCD, 2.8 in (71 mm), 224x144 pixels, 241 colors * Sound: One mono speaker, optional headphones (mute, soft, loud settings) * Link: Link adapter required for two players * Power: 20 hours of gameplay on one AA battery * Size: 128 mm x 743 mm x 24.3 mm * Weight: 95 g (3.35 oz)

{{Platform |Name=XaviXPORT |Guid=3045-132 |Aliases=XaviX Port |Image=https://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/scale_super/0/4647/2954582-xavix-xavixport-console-fl.jpg |Deck=The XaviXPORT is a fitness-oriented system that works with cartridges and fitness gear, like specialized golf clubs, boxing clubs, and baseball bats. |ShortName=XAVX |ReleaseDate=2004-08-28 |ReleaseDateType=Month |OnlineSupport=No |OriginalPrice=79.99 |Manufacturer=Companies/SSD_Company_Limited }} <div id='imageData' data-json='{"infobox":{"file":"2954582-xavix-xavixport-console-fl.jpg","path":"0\/4647\/","mime":"image\/jpeg","sizes":"scale_super,square_small,square_medium,scale_small,square_tiny,square_mini,scale_medium,square_avatar,scale_large,scale_avatar,screen_medium,screen_kubrick","caption":null},"background":[]}' />
==Overview == The XaviXPORT, released in August of 2004 by a [[Locations/Japan|Japanese]] company called [[Companies/SSD_Company_Limited|SSD Company Limited]], is a fitness based system, employing the usage of [[Concepts/Motion_Control|motion controls]] in an effort to get players off of the couch. The system itself is fairly unconventional, not only in size (it is much smaller than even the slim [[Platforms/PlayStation_2|PlayStation 2]]) and technical specifications, but also in the games themselves. The console anticipated [[Companies/Nintendo|Nintendo]]'s [[Platforms/Wii|Wii]] by two years. http://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/original/0/7465/1745891-04_xavixport_golf_ss.jpg In terms of hardware, the XaviXPORT is incredibly simple, containing minimal processors and low memory. The cartridges actually contain the XaviX Multiprocessor, a proprietary chip. Some games contain the Super XavIX chip, an upgraded version of the original, and SSD is reportedly working on a newer chip titled XaviX II. The console does not actually contain any controller ports. Rather, the games come with dedicated controllers. Each game comes with a specialized controller, such as a golf club for the [[Games/Golf_34605|Golf]] game. Games retail at $49.99 USD. The XaviXPORT was actually developed by eight of the engineers who worked on the original development of the [[Platforms/Nintendo_Entertainment_System|Nintendo Entertainment System]], which explains the CPU (6502), as well as the Super XaviX's CPU (65816), both of which were used in Nintendo consoles (6502 on the NES and 65816 on the [[Platforms/Super_Nintendo_Entertainment_System|SNES]]). Also of note is the fact that [[People/Jackie_Chan|Jackie Chan]] partnered with SSD Company Limited in order to bring his likeness to the console. There are two games coming to the XaviXPORT with his name attached: Jackie Chan's Action Boxing and Jackie Chan's J-Mat.
==Hardware Specifications == * '''Processor''': 8-bit CPU (6502), 21 MHz (multiprocessors contained in the games themselves) * '''Screen Resolution''': 640 x 480 with 16.7 million colors * '''Audio''': 16-bit, 44 kHz Memory: 1 KB RAM * '''Ports''': One USB port (no controller ports, these are included with the games)

{{Platform |Name=Xbox |Guid=3045-32 |Aliases=MARZ,VERV,DirectX Box |Image=https://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/scale_super/0/3699/359843-xbox.jpg |Deck=Microsoft's first home gaming system and one of the first to include an internal hard drive and built in online play capability. It was considered the first console to have fully supported meaningful online play. |ShortName=XBOX |ReleaseDate=2001-11-15 |ReleaseDateType=Full |InstallBase=24,650,000 |OnlineSupport=Yes |OriginalPrice=299.99 |Manufacturer=Companies/Xbox_Game_Studios }} <div id='imageData' data-json='{"infobox":{"file":"359843-xbox.jpg","path":"0\/3699\/","mime":"image\/jpeg","sizes":"square_tiny,square_medium,scale_super,scale_small,square_small,square_avatar,scale_medium,screen_medium,scale_large,square_mini,scale_avatar,ignore_jpg_scale_medium,scale_tiny,screen_kubrick","caption":""},"background":{"file":"1536200-xbox800.jpg","path":"7\/72276\/","mime":"image\/jpeg","sizes":"square_small,square_tiny,square_medium,screen_kubrick_wide,scale_tiny,scale_super,scale_large,scale_small,scale_medium,square_mini,scale_avatar,screen_kubrick,screen_medium,square_avatar","caption":"Early prototype\/mockup\/display model"}}' />
==Overview== After seeing how successful the gaming market was, [[Companies/Xbox_Game_Studios|Microsoft]] decided it was time to produce a home console; The Xbox (which originally was going to be called the DirectX-box) was released in North America on November 15, 2001, Japan on February 22, and Europe and Australia on March 14, 2002. It was the direct competitor of the [[Platforms/GameCube|Nintendo GameCube]] and the [[Platforms/PlayStation_2|PlayStation 2]]. It was the first home console on the market to include an internal hard drive by default. This was a breakthrough at the time as it made games run faster that used the HDD and also allowed players to save their progress without the use of a Memory Card. The Xbox shipped with a custom made Intel processor based on the Intel III architecture. This processor allowed faster calculations resulting in a much more realistic gaming experience at the time. nVidia produced the Xbox’s GPU which allowed better graphics than other consoles at the time. https://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/original/1/15553/820253-original_controller.jpg When first released, the controller (with the exception of the controller found in Japan) was criticized for being abnormally large compared to its competitors. Eventually, it became known as "The Fatty" or "The Duke" and was replaced with the smaller controller sold in Japan, known as the "Controller S" or "S Type". The system was succeeded by the [[Platforms/Xbox_360|Xbox 360]], which was released in late 2005. By the end of 2005, production of the original Xbox had ceased. The final game for the system was [[Games/Madden_NFL_09|Madden NFL 09]], released on August 12th 2008.
==Launch Titles==
==='''U.S.'''=== * [[Games/Halo_Combat_Evolved|Halo: Combat Evolved]] * [[Games/Oddworld_Munchs_Oddysee|Oddworld: Munch's Oddysee]] * [[Games/Dead_or_Alive_3|Dead or Alive 3]] * [[Franchises/Project_Gotham_Racing|Project Gotham Racing]] * [[Games/NFL_Fever_2002|NFL Fever 2002]] * [[Games/AirForce_Delta_Storm|Air Force Delta Storm]] * [[Games/Mad_Dash_Racing|Mad Dash Racing]] * [[Games/Cel_Damage|Cel Damage]] * [[Games/Arctic_Thunder|Arctic Thunder]] * [[Games/Fuzion_Frenzy|Fuzion Frenzy]] * [[Games/Tony_Hawks_Pro_Skater_2x|Tony Hawk's Pro Skater 2X]]
==='''Japan'''=== * * [[Games/Wreckless_The_Yakuza_Missions|Double S.T.E.A.L.]]
==='''Europe'''=== * [[Games/Amped_Freestyle_Snowboarding|Amped: Freestyle Snowboarding]] * [[Games/Batman_Vengeance|Batman: Vengeance]] * [[Games/Blood_Wake|Blood Wake]] * [[Games/Dave_Mirra_Freestyle_BMX_2|Dave Mirra Freestyle BMX 2]] * [[Games/Dead_or_Alive_3|Dead or Alive 3]] * [[Games/Fuzion_Frenzy|Fuzion Frenzy]] * [[Games/Halo_Combat_Evolved|Halo: Combat Evolved]] * [[Games/JSRF_Jet_Set_Radio_Future|Jet Set Radio Future]] * [[Games/Mad_Dash_Racing|Mad Dash Racing]] * [[Games/Max_Payne|Max Payne]] * [[Games/NBA_Live_2002|NBA Live 2002]] * [[Games/NHL_2002|NHL 2002]] * [[Games/NHL_Hitz_20_02|NHL Hitz 2002]] * [[Games/Oddworld_Munchs_Oddysee|Oddworld: Munch's Oddysee]] * [[Franchises/Project_Gotham_Racing|Project Gotham Racing]] * [[Games/RalliSport_Challenge|RalliSport Challenge]] * [[Games/Tony_Hawks_Pro_Skater_3|Tony Hawk's Pro Skater 3]] * [[Games/TransWorld_SURF|TransWorld Surf]] * [[Games/Wreckless_The_Yakuza_Missions|Wreckless: The Yakuza Missions]]
==Innovation== Xbox was the first console to include a built in hard drive. Users were able to rip music from their CD collection and listen from the Xbox's hard drive. This also made custom in-game soundtracks possible. The first game to take advantage of this was [[Companies/Rockstar_Games|Rockstar]]'s [[Grand Theft Auto Double Pack.|Grand Theft Auto Double Pack.]] The Xbox was the first console to charge an annual fee to play online and introduced fully integrated online gaming on home consoles with the introduction of [[Concepts/Xbox_Live|Xbox Live]].
==Sales== When the Xbox launched on November 21, 2001, the console quickly sold out in North America. In Japan, however, the console sold only 250,000 units. After its release in November 2004, [[Games/Halo_2|Halo 2]] became the best selling Xbox title worldwide, selling over 8 million copies, thanks in large part to the quality of its online multiplayer through Xbox Live. According to Microsoft the Xbox has sold over 24 million worldwide as of 2006.
==Support== Microsoft officially no longer offers on the Xbox after March 2, 2009. Microsoft is rumored to offer an Xbox 360 upgrade offer to users of the original Xbox. Real support for the Xbox ended on November 22, 2005 with the launch of the console's successor, the [[Platforms/Xbox_360|Xbox 360]]. [[Concepts/Xbox_Live|Xbox Live]] support for the Xbox was discontinued for all Xbox original games on April 15th 2010.
==Technical Specifications==
===Dimensions=== * 320 x 100 x 260 mm (12.5 x 4 x 10.5 in)
===Weight=== * 2.4 kg (5 lbs. 5 oz.)
===Media=== * 2-5x DVD * 10 GB hard disk * 8 MB memory card
===Interfaces=== * 4 Controller ports * 2 Memory Card slots * AV Multi Out connector * 2 USB connectors * i.Link (TM (IEEE1394 connector)
===Features=== * An internal hard drive, for storage of game information -- a first in the console gaming industry. The Hard Drive also could store music for use in some games. * An Intel 733MHz processor, the most powerful CPU of any console at the time of launch. * An NVIDIA graphics processing unit (GPU, delivering more than three times the graphics performance of other consoles. * An Ethernet port for fast-action online gaming via a broadband connection. * A multisignal audio-video connector that allows for easy hookup to televisions and home theater systems. * A front-loading slide-out DVD tray. * Four game controller ports that allow easy multiplayer gaming and enable other peripherals, ranging from game pads to voice-activated headsets.

{{Platform |Name=Xbox 360 |Guid=3045-20 |Aliases=360,Xenon,X360 |Image=https://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/scale_super/0/1992/3792703-0260626949-05051.jpg |Deck=The Xbox 360 is the second game console produced by Microsoft Corporation and is the successor to the original Xbox. |ShortName=X360 |ReleaseDate=2005-11-22 |InstallBase=85,700,000 |OnlineSupport=Yes |OriginalPrice=399.99 |Manufacturer=Companies/Xbox_Game_Studios }} <div id='imageData' data-json='{"infobox":{"file":"3792703-0260626949-05051.jpg","path":"0\/1992\/","mime":"image\/jpeg","sizes":"scale_super,square_medium,square_mini,square_small,scale_small,square_tiny,scale_medium,square_avatar,screen_medium,scale_large,scale_avatar,screen_kubrick,scale_tiny","caption":null},"background":{"file":"204093-poweredye6.png","path":"0\/611\/","mime":"image\/png","sizes":"scale_medium,scale_super,square_mini,square_medium,square_small,screen_medium,square_tiny,scale_kubrick,screen_kubrick_wide,scale_tiny,square_avatar,screen_kubrick,scale_large,scale_avatar,scale_small","caption":""}}' />
===Xbox 360 Premium=== The Xbox 360 Premium was identical to the Xbox 360 Pro configuration but contained an older CPU architecture. Manufacturing for the Premium branded units ceased in August 2007.
===Xbox 360 Core=== https://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/original/2/26636/2352653-xbox360_core1.jpg The Xbox 360 Core was the barebones 360 model that carried an MSRP of $279.99 and was similar to the current Arcade configuration. It included a wired controller, had no HDMI port, and did not include any kind of storage. The Xbox 360 Core was often seen as a misstep because its production meant that developers could not take advantage of the features Xbox 360 HDD in their games, since not all users would have one. It's main benefit to Microsoft was in marketing the platform when it was launched, as they were able to state that consumers could buy an Xbox 360 for "under $300," even though the more desirable Xbox 360 Premium was close to $400 USD at the time.
===Xbox 360 Pro=== https://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/original/2/26636/2352652-xbox_3602.jpg The Xbox 360 Pro, available in the "Go Pro" bundle, had an MSRP of $249.99 USD and replaced the Xbox 360 Premium SKU. Included in the bundle were all the features found in the Xbox 360 Arcade, as well as a 60 GB hard drive and a hybrid composite/component cable. The pre-loaded, detachable 60GB hard drive came with game demos, video clips and a free Live Arcade game: Hexic HD. The hard drive allowed for the storage of downloaded content, backwards compatibility with original Xbox games, and storage of game saves. The Xbox 360 Pro bundle carried an MSRP of $299.99 USD. Prior to August 2008, the included HDD was only 20GB. For the 2007 holiday shopping season, Microsoft bundled copies of [[Games/Forza_Motorsport_2|Forza Motorsport 2]] and [[Games/Marvel_Ultimate_Alliance|Marvel: Ultimate Alliance]] with the Xbox 360 Pro. In 2008, the holiday bundle included [[Games/LEGO_Indiana_Jones_The_Original_Adventures|Lego Indiana Jones]] and [[Games/Kung_Fu_Panda|Kung Fu Panda]]. In 2009, reduced the price of the Xbox 360 Elite to $299.99, and phased out the Xbox 360 Pro.
===Xbox 360 Arcade=== An entry level Xbox 360 that replaced the now discontinued Xbox 360 Core. The Xbox 360 Arcade sports a wireless controller, 256 MB memory unit (Which has now been replaced with an internal 256mb chip in the 'Jasper' version of consoles), composite AV cable, HDMI 1.2 output, and 5 Xbox Live Arcade titles: [[Games/Boom_Boom_Rocket|Boom Boom Rocket]], [[Games/Feeding_Frenzy|Feeding Frenzy]], [[Games/Luxor_2|Luxor 2]], [[Games/Pac_Man_Championship_Edition|Pac-Man Championship Edition]], and [[Games/Uno|Uno]] on a single disk. This disc also includes a "Welcome Video" and several game trailers/demos. Officially announce on October 22, 2007, the Xbox 360 Arcade has a MSRP of $199.99 USD. Recently Microsoft confirmed that they are now putting internal memory into the Xbox 360 Arcade. This storage will be the same size as the previous external memory units: 256MB. Xbox 360 Arcade systems packaged for the 2008 holiday shopping season are bundled with a copy of [[Games/Sega_Superstars_Tennis|Sega Superstars Tennis]].
===Xbox 360 Elite=== https://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/original/2/26636/2352654-xbox360_elite_04.jpg The Elite is currently the mid-end Xbox 360 configuration carrying an MSRP of $249.99 USD. It has all of the features of the Xbox 360 Pro, but comes with an upgraded 120 GB hard drive and has a matte black finish with matching controller and headset. For the 2008 holiday shopping season, The Elite model came packed with[[Games/LEGO_Indiana_Jones_The_Original_Adventures|Lego Indiana Jones]] and [[Games/Kung_Fu_Panda|Kung Fu Panda]]. In 2009 there was a bundle which included both [[Games/Fable_II|Fable 2]] and [[Games/Halo_3|Halo 3]]. This was followed with a bundle containing [[Games/LEGO_Batman_The_Videogame|Lego Batman]] and [[Games/Pure|Pure]]. In 2009, Microsoft dropped the price of the Xbox 360 Elite to $299.99, most likely to match the price of Sony's new PS3 slim carrying the same price. In 2010, Microsoft introduced a redesigned Xbox 360 and reduced the price of the 360 Elite to $249.99.
===Xbox 360 Splinter Cell Conviction Bundle=== Released with little to no fanfare, the [[Games/Tom_Clancys_Splinter_Cell_Conviction|Splinter Cell Conviction]] bundle is quite similar to its immediate predecessor, the [[Games/Final_Fantasy_XIII|Final Fantasy XIII]] bundle. It includes a black Xbox 360 Console, a 250GB HDD, two black Xbox 360 controllers, and a copy of [[Franchises/Tom_Clancy|Tom Clancy]]'s Splinter Cell Conviction. Unlike many of the special edition Xbox 360 bundles, this one does not feature any unique artwork or designs on any of the included hardware. It retailed for $399.99 US.
==Games== The Xbox 360 has a large array of games starting in November 2005. On launch, Microsoft's first party titles included [[Games/Perfect_Dark_Zero|Perfect Dark Zero]], [[Games/Kameo_Elements_of_Power|Kameo: Elements of Power]], and [[Games/Project_Gotham_Racing_3|PGR 3]]. Activision's [[Games/Call_of_Duty_2|Call of Duty 2]] introduces console gamers to the series and became the biggest game on Xbox Live during launch. Later in 2006, Epic Games released [[Games/Gears_of_War_2|Gears of War]], what was considered to be the "killer app" of the Xbox 360. Thanks to the game's impressive graphics and multiplayer, the game became a big hit. 2007 would follow many games like [[Games/Halo_3|Halo 3]] (one of the most hyped games of the year), [[Games/Mass_Effect|Mass Effect]], and [[Games/Call_of_Duty_4_Modern_Warfare|Call of Duty 4: Modern Warfare]], which broke many records on Xbox Live when it was released, with its [[Games/Call_of_Duty_Modern_Warfare_2|sequel]] surpassing it. Many other sequels would be released including [[Games/Fable_II|Fable 2]] and [[Games/Banjo_Kazooie_Nuts_And_Bolts|Banjo Kazooie: Nuts and Bolts]] within the later years. One key feature to Xbox Live's success is the Xbox Live Arcade games. On launch, the 360 Premium bundle came packaged with[[Games/Hexic|Hexic HD]], a puzzle game created by [[Franchises/Tetris|Tetris]] creator [[People/Alexey_Pajitnov|Alexey Pajitnov]]. Other launch titles include[[Games/Geometry_Wars_Retro_Evolved|Geometry Wars: Retro Evolved]] and[[Games/Marble_Blast_Ultra|Marble Blast Ultra]]. In the beginning, Microsoft restricted Arcade game sizes to no more than 50 MB big due to the Core model and its memory card. Later, when the 256 MB memory card was released, the game size limit was expanded. Today you're able to get full games online and games can be installed to your hard drive.
===Compatibility with original Xbox Games=== By and large the Xbox 360 is not compatible with original Xbox games out of the box. Instead Microsoft provided software emulation for selected titles. The emulation is based on games with similar architecture to each other. This means that from a distance the list of compatible titles may look strange with such inclusions as [[Games/Barbie_Horse_Adventure_Wild_Horse_Rescue|Barbie's Horse Adventure]] while many best selling or critically acclaimed titles are seemingly ignored. The emulation is not perfect and in a lot of case games feature glitches that were not present in the original version. Glitches can go from minor (slowdown, framerate issues) to sever (some glitches cause games to full on crash) Thirteen games were pulled from the service presumable due to such issue. (Seven of which were later reinstated) Microsoft stopped updating the Xbox emulator in 2007. According to Wikipedia the final total of backwards compatible titles is 476 title making up approximately half of all Xbox games..
==New Xbox Experience (NXE)== https://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/original/0/1694/365903-360_dashboard.jpg Since launch the main way of interacting with the Xbox 360 has been using the 'Dashboard', a menu system split into sections by 'Blades'; initially the 'Dashboard' consisted of four 'Blades:' Live, Games, Media and Settings; a blade was later added which separated the Xbox Live Marketplace from the Xbox Live 'Blade'. At the 2008 Electronic Entertainment Expo (E3) Microsoft announced it would be completely redesigning the Xbox Dashboard into a more conventional menu system dubbed the [[Concepts/New_Xbox_Experience|New Xbox Experience]]. The New Xbox Experience (NXE) is designed to be more user friendly and to be expandable as new content is added, it takes several design cues from the likes of Apple and Nintendo, and introduces several new features such as Avatars and Live Party. The New Xbox Experience was released on November 19, 2008.
===Notable features of the NXE:=== https://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/original/0/9734/769073-3.jpg * Avatars: A 3D representation of the user, replaces the current Gamercard; however Microsoft assure gamers they can still use their old Gamercard. * Live Party: A group of up to 8 people, allows users to voice chat independent of which game they are each playing. * Play From Drive: Allows players to copy disc content over to the HDD in order to cut down load times and DVD drive noise. The disc of the game played from the HDD is still required to be in the DVD drive. * Deleting Zero-Point games: The ability to delete games with no achievements unlocked has become possible, so that players may improve their ratio of possible achievements to ones gained. * Netflix: The Xbox 360 gained the ability to stream movies from Netfilx to view on your TV. This feature is only available to gold members and requires a Netflix paid account. The new Xbox 360 Guide (accessible through the Xbox button on the controller) will mimic the interface of the "Blade" dashboard system. It's accessible in all Xbox 360 games to manage messages/invites, create parties, view player statistics, et cetera. The NXE update was distributed in the fall of 2008, and took 128 Mb of space to install. Those not meeting this requirement (The Arcade and Core SKUs not having hard drives) were given a free memory unit (512MB) by Microsoft or the option to buy a 20GB Hard Drive at $20.
===Features after NXE:=== At CES 2009 Microsoft's Robby Bach announced [[Concepts/Xbox_Live_Primetime|Xbox Live Primetime]]. Primetime was the first channel on Xbox Live to use scheduled programming at specific time so you can find friends & experience live events. One of the first games to run in Primetime was [[Games/1_vs_100|1 VS 100]], which has since been discontinued. On November 17, 2009, Microsoft released another new update for the Xbox 360. With this new update, Microsoft made available custom hubs for [[Concepts/Facebook_Connectivity|Facebook]] and [[Concepts/Twitter_Support|Twitter]], along with added features like posting pictures of your games on your [[Concepts/Facebook_Connectivity|Facebook]] page. Other features include Last.fm, Zune HD, and accessing news stations like MSNBC.
==Criticisms== There are a number of criticism that are often leveraged against the Xbox 360, the main one being unreliable hardware quality; however, excessive DVD drive noise and a confusing amount of hardware configurations are also targeted by critics of the system.
==='The Red Ring of Death'=== https://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/original/0/1694/326567-red_ring_of_death_1_.jpg The Xbox 360 has gained a reputation for a high failure rate, sometimes rumored to be as high as 30% although Microsoft have denied the failure rate is that high; they have not released an official failure rate. The most common hardware failure is the General Hardware Failure, denoted by the bottom two and upper right quadrant of the Ring of Light flashing red; leading to the failure being dubbed the ' [[Red Ring Of Death|Red Ring of Death]]'. This failure renders the console unable to boot or 'bricked' and the only official course of action for the consumer being to call Xbox support to ship the console back to Microsoft where it is either repaired or replaced. While there is no official course of action on fixing the Red Ring of Death there are some unofficial solutions to try. The most popular is called the "towel trick" in which you take everything off of your Xbox 360, your hard-drive, A/V cables, etc. The only thing you want plugged in is the power supply. Now you wrap it in three (3) large towels (the ones you would use to dry yourself off after a shower) and turn it on. Let it sit running for 20-30 minutes, turn it off, and let it cool off for another 15. Then turn it on and it should work. *Note this is not a permanent solution.* No official reason has been given by Microsoft as to why this error is so common place, however most speculation points towards insufficient cooling around the GPU leading to a warping of the motherboard. Microsoft has taken steps to solve this problem by adding an additional heat sink to the GPU in later units and units returned from repair. These changes were included in a chipset refresh codenamed 'Falcon' which also includes a 65nm CPU to further reduce heat. An as yet unreleased chip set refresh, codenamed 'Jasper,' will introduce a 65nm GPU and is hoped to solve the 'Red Ring of Death' problem. As well as these hardware revisions Microsoft has increased the Xbox 360 warranty to 3 years, however this is only to cover the 'Red Ring of Death' all other hardware problems are only covered for 1 year; this was reported to have cost Microsoft $1 billion. ''Note'': the new "XBOX 360 'S'" does not feature the red LEDs behind the power button. Microsoft removed this particular error message because of the bad connotations associated with this.
===DVD Drive Problems=== The DVD Drive is often the target of criticism, mostly for the excessive noise produced and an apparent tendency for scratching discs. These issues are less apparent in later models of the 360 as Microsoft have replaced the initial DVD drive with a quieter model from BenQ.
===Power Adapter Size=== The Xbox 360's power adapter is significantly larger than similar adapters on previous consoles. This criticism was mostly vocal at the beginning of the Xbox 360's retail life, as Microsoft has reduced the power adapter's size over the life of the console.
===Xbox Live Arcade File Size Restriction=== Initially Xbox Live Arcade games had to come in under the 50 MB file size restriction. While many people felt that that 50MB was too small to create games this limitation allowed 360 owners without the hard drive to enjoy all of the content of XBLA at that time. Over time Microsoft has relaxed restrictions to coincide with the release of larger memory units. Currently the size limit is 2GB and this is a limitation of the backend of service itself rather than an arbitrary limit imposed by Microsoft.

==Overview== The Xbox Live Games Store, formerly the Xbox Live Marketplace, allows [[Platforms/Xbox_360|Xbox 360]] and [[Platforms/Xbox_One|Xbox One]] users to download different kinds of games. Downloads were originally purchased using Microsoft points, but were changed to real currency prior to the launch of Xbox One. The Marketplace also allows players to download add-ons to their games; maps, extra episodes or levels, music tracks etc. The majority of the downloads are stored on the 360’s hard drive. On November 19, 2008, Microsoft released the [[Concepts/New_Xbox_Experience|New Xbox Experience]], which completely overhauled the Xbox 360 Dashboard, and added several new features. The storefront was closed on July 29th, 2024, resulting in an inability to purchase any content on the store as well as delisting hundreds of games and DLC. The Xbox 360 marketplace was suceeded by the Xbox Store in 2017. Backwards compatible games and DLC can still be bought on the current Xbox Store and games previously purchased can still be downloaded and played indefinitely.
==Xbox 360== https://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/original/0/8272/1145529-xbox_live_marketplace.jpg Originally, during the [[Platforms/Xbox_360|Xbox 360]] era, the Xbox Live Marketplace was split into three sections: Xbox Live Arcade, Xbox Live Indie Games and Games on Demand (retail games). There is also a [[Games/Game_Room|Game Room]] application that lets you download and play old games through the application that must be downloaded through the marketplace.
===Xbox Live Arcade=== https://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/original/0/1239/425389-xbla_logo.jpg Xbox Live Arcade is where game publishers release download-only games. It started with mostly rereleases of classic arcade titles like [[Games/Gauntlet|Gauntlet]], [[Games/Smash_TV|Smash TV]], and [[Games/Frogger|Frogger]], but later expanded to expanded to new, original titles like [[Games/Shadow_Complex|Shadow Complex]], [[Games/Castle_Crashers|Castle Crashers]], and [[Games/Brothers_A_Tale_of_Two_Sons|Brothers: A Tale of Two Sons]].
===Games on Demand=== [[Concepts/Games_on_Demand|Games on Demand]] allows users to purchase retail Xbox 360 games as well as original [[Platforms/Xbox|Xbox]] games that have been made [[Concepts/Backwards_Compatibility|backwards compatible]]. Certain games have been released simultaneously at retail and on [[Concepts/Games_on_Demand|Games on Demand]] (i.e.[[Games/Crackdown_2|Crackdown 2]]), however there is usually a delay between the retail release and the digital release.
===Xbox Live Indie Games=== [[Concepts/Xbox_Live_Indie_Games|Xbox Live Indie Games]] are games made by independent developers and are self-published onto the marketplace. The price of these games are generally cheaper, but they are not rated by the ESRB nor does Microsoft ensure the quality of the game.
===De-listed Xbox 360 Games=== Some games have been de-listed from the marketplace, here's a list of them all. These games are still available to download from your purchase history if you purchased them before, however they can no longer be purchased. * [[Games/1_vs_100|1 vs 100]] * [[Games/Are_You_Smarter_Than_A_5th_Grader_Make_the_Grade|Are You Smarter than A 5th Grader? Make The Grade]] * [[Games/Battlestar_Galactica|Battlestar Galactica]] * [[Games/Chessmaster_Live|Chessmaster LIVE]] * [[Games/Coffeetime_Crosswords|Coffeetime Crosswords]] * [[Games/Tournament_Cyberball_2072|Cyberball 2072]] * [[Games/Dash_of_Destruction|Dash of Destruction]] * [[Games/Diner_Dash|Diner Dash]] * [[Games/Double_D_Dodgeball|Double D Dodgeball]] * [[Games/Double_Dragon_1987|Double Dragon]] * [[Games/Dream_Chronicles|Dream Chronicles]] * [[Games/Dungeons_And_Dragons_Daggerdale|Dungeons and Dragons: Daggerdale]] * [[Games/Football_Genius_The_Ultimate_Quiz|Football Genius: The Ultimate Quiz]] * [[Games/Gauntlet|Gauntlet]] * [[Games/Ion_Assault|Ion Assault]] * [[Games/Inferno_Pool|Inferno Pool]] * [[Games/Joust|Joust]] * [[Games/Kung_Fu_Strike_The_Warriors_Rise|Kung Fu Strike: The Warrior's Rise]] * [[Games/Live_Draft_Tracker|Live Draft Tracker]] * [[Games/Live_Score_Tracker|Live Score Tracker]] * [[Games/Lost_Cities|Lost Cities]] * [[Games/Lucha_Fury|Lucha Fury]] * [[Games/Marble_Blast_Ultra|Marble Blast Ultra]] * [[Games/Merv_Griffins_Crosswords|Merv Griffin's Crosswords]] * [[Games/NBA_2K10_Draft_Combine|NBA 2K10 Draft Combine]] * [[Games/NBA_Unrivaled|NBA Unrivaled]] * [[Games/OutRun_Online_Arcade|Outrun Online Arcade]] * [[Games/Paperboy|Paperboy]] * [[Games/Pinball_FX|Pinball FX]] * [[Games/Robotron_2084|Robotron 2084]] * [[Games/Roogoo|Roogoo]] * [[Games/Root_Beer_Tapper|Root Beer Tapper]] * [[Games/Screwjumper|Screwjumper!]] * [[Games/Sega_Rally_Online_Arcade|Sega Rally Online Arcade]] * [[Games/Smash_TV|Smash TV]] * [[Games/Speedball_2_Brutal_Deluxe|Speedball 2]] * [[Games/Teenage_Mutant_Ninja_Turtles_Turtles_in_Time|TMNT: Turtles in Time Re-Shelled]] * [[Games/TotemBall|TotemBall]] * [[Games/Ultimate_Mortal_Kombat_3|Ultimate Mortal Kombat 3]] * [[Games/Yaris|Yaris]]
